description:
You are acting as the Release-Engineering assistant for a medium-sized SaaS company.  
The Release Manager has asked you to produce a concise deployment-frequency report for the first week of August 2023 so that they can quickly identify which service versions were deployed most often.

Current set-up
1. A directory named “/home/user/releases” already exists and is writable by the current user.  
2. Inside that directory there is an uncompressed plain-text file called “deployment_history_2023-08.log”.  
   • Each line in the log contains three whitespace-separated fields in the exact order  
     “YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS <service>:<version>”, e.g.  
     2023-08-02 07:01:11 payments:3.1.0  
   • There are no blank lines and there is no header row.  
   • Timestamps are unique; the <service>:<version> string may repeat.

Requested deliverable
Create a new tab-separated values (TSV) file named

    /home/user/releases/version_frequency_report.tsv

that satisfies ALL of the following rules:

A. The report must contain one header line followed by the aggregated data lines.  
B. The header line must be exactly (case-sensitive)  
       artifact<TAB>deployments  
   where <TAB> represents a single horizontal TAB character (ASCII 0x09).  
C. For every distinct “<service>:<version>” token found in the source log, count how many times that token occurs.  
D. Produce one output line per distinct token using the format  
       <service>:<version><TAB><count>  
E. Sort the data lines in strictly decreasing numeric order of <count>.  
   • Whenever two or more artifacts have the same count, order those tied artifacts lexicographically (ascending, byte-wise) by the entire “<service>:<version>” string.  
F. Make sure there are no leading or trailing spaces on any line.  
G. The final file must end with a single UNIX line-feed (LF, 0x0A) character.

Hints (do not violate any rule above)  
• A typical UNIX one-liner for this kind of problem involves a pipeline of “cut | sort | uniq -c | sort -k1,1nr -k2,2”.  
• Remember to convert the “uniq -c” output into pure “count<TAB>artifact” or “artifact<TAB>count” as required.  
• Validate the final ordering manually; automated tests will do a byte-for-byte comparison against the expected file.  
• Do NOT remove, rename or modify the original “deployment_history_2023-08.log”.  
• Ensure the new report lives exactly at the path given above.  
• You are free to create temporary files in “/home/user/releases” but they will not be checked and may be deleted after execution.

Acceptance criteria checked by automation
1. “/home/user/releases/version_frequency_report.tsv” exists.  
2. The file is world-readable (0600 ≤ mode ≤ 0644).  
3. The first line is the correct header.  
4. Every subsequent line contains exactly one TAB separating the artifact and the count, with both fields non-empty and the count a positive integer written without commas or other formatting.  
5. The lines appear in the order described in Rule E.  
6. The final line ends with a single LF and there are no blank lines anywhere.  
7. The numeric counts match those in the source log, meaning that the test harness will independently recompute counts and compare.

Your task: produce that report. Work entirely from the Linux command line; no manual file editing is permitted. Once you believe the report is correct, you’re done.
truth:
Initial file that already exists before the agent starts:

Path: /home/user/releases/deployment_history_2023-08.log  
Permissions: 0644  
Exact byte contents (including terminating LF):

2023-08-01 04:15:22 myservice:1.4.0
2023-08-01 05:00:41 auth:2.3.1
2023-08-02 06:32:10 myservice:1.4.0
2023-08-02 07:01:11 payments:3.1.0
2023-08-03 08:15:22 myservice:1.4.2
2023-08-03 09:36:15 auth:2.3.1
2023-08-04 10:44:55 payments:3.1.0
2023-08-04 11:22:33 payments:3.1.0
2023-08-05 12:30:45 myservice:1.4.2
2023-08-05 13:11:09 auth:2.3.2

(Ends with one newline character.)

Correct aggregated counts derived from the file:

payments:3.1.0 → 3  
auth:2.3.1    → 2  
myservice:1.4.0 → 2  
myservice:1.4.2 → 2  
auth:2.3.2    → 1  

Therefore the reference answer file must be exactly:

artifact<TAB>deployments\n
payments:3.1.0<TAB>3\n
auth:2.3.1<TAB>2\n
myservice:1.4.0<TAB>2\n
myservice:1.4.2<TAB>2\n
auth:2.3.2<TAB>1\n

(where <TAB> is the ASCII horizontal tab 0x09 and \n is the single LF 0x0A).  
Path for the answer file that the agent must create: /home/user/releases/version_frequency_report.tsv  
Permissions that will be accepted: 0644 (or stricter, e.g. 0600).

Automated grading will compare the file byte-for-byte against the above reference.
